CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-25-2013 Continued To Jun-19-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Hearing To Obtain Default Interlocutory Judgment For Partition
Off Calendar May-07-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing on Interlocutory Judgment for Partition.
Dept. 514, Judge: Paul Alvarado, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Sonia Wong, #11288.

Ian E. Anderson, Esq., appearing for Plaintiffs Keiko Hattori, Kathy Fujimoto, and Margaret Vintero.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) Kiyoko Takata
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.
Keiko Hattori sworn and examinded on her own behalf.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court enters Interlocutory judgment as follows:
1. Each Plaintiff owns one sixth of the real property that is commonly known as 2133 Golden Gate Avenue, San Francisco, California.
2. The Defendant Kiyoko Takata owns the remaining half of 2133 Golden Gate.
3. Home Auxiliary Corporation, a California corporation potentially has a lien against 2133 Golden Gate by operation of its deed of trust recorded on September 29, 1952.
4. Under the circumstances, sale of the property and division of the proceeds would be more equitable than division of the property; the Court therefore orders that the property be sold and the proceeds divided among the parties according to their respective interests:
5. Anne Kennedy is appointed as referee with authority to sell the property at a private sale. this manner of sale is determine by the Court to be the most beneficial to the parties. Notice of sale shall be given to the persons specified in Code of Civil Procedure 873.600 873.690.
6. Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 872.630, the referee shall ascertain the facts necessary for the determination of whether the deed of trust recorded for the benefit of Home Auxiliary Corporation, a California corporation, is still valid and enforceable. And to the extent that if the lien is still enforceable, the referee shall ascertain the facts necessary for the determination of how much of the sale proceeds should go toward the satisfaction and release of that lien.
7. The referee, after selling the property, is directed to report to this Court on the sale proceedings and, on confirmation of the sale by this Court and on payment of the purchase price, is authorized and directed to execute and deliver a deed of the property to the buyer.
8. Unless modified by subsequent order of this Court, the proceeds from the sale of the property shall be applied, after confirmation of the sale as follows:
a. To pay the expenses of sale
b. To pay other costs of partition or secure any cost of partition later allowed:
c. The residue shall be distributed among the parties in proportion to their shares-one
sixth to each Plaintiff and one half to the Defendant Kiyoko Takata.
9. This Court retains jurisdiction to review the referee's report on the property sale and to order equitable adjustments that the Court considers just and appropriate.
Court signed Default Interloxutory Judgment for Partition in open court.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-23-2013 Continued To May-08-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
